task_description:
Screen these abstracts for a systematic review on ML methods for automated skin lesion diagnosis from dermoscopy or clinical photographs. Produce a ranked top-5 shortlist.

INCLUDE only papers that present or evaluate an ML model classifying skin lesions (benign vs malignant, or multiclass dermatological diagnosis) from dermoscopy images, clinical photographs, or reflectance confocal microscopy. Papers about non-skin conditions, non-dermoscopy imaging, prognosis, treatment selection, or methodology without skin lesion diagnosis should be EXCLUDE.

For each INCLUDE paper: assign rank 1 (best) to 5 (fifth-best) and write one justification sentence (max 200 chars).

Quality rubric: 1=no method; 2=vague; 3=clear metrics; 4=multiple baselines, external validation, and ablation.

papers.title: EfficientNet-B5 for 8-Class Skin Lesion Diagnosis on ISIC 2019
papers.abstract: We fine-tune EfficientNet-B5 on 25,000 dermoscopy images from ISIC 2019 for 8-class skin lesion classification including melanoma, BCC, and seborrhoeic keratosis. Balanced accuracy 0.88 is achieved, outperforming the challenge winner and ResNet-50, DenseNet-121, and ViT-B baselines. Calibration curves per class and performance stratified by Fitzpatrick skin type are provided. Code is released.
papers.topic_hint: EfficientNet, dermoscopy, ISIC 2019, 8-class diagnosis, balanced accuracy
papers.methodology_hint: EfficientNet-B5, ISIC 2019 25k, balanced accuracy 0.88, 3 baselines, skin type stratification, code
papers.claimed_contribution: EfficientNet for 8-class dermoscopy diagnosis balanced accuracy 0.88, Fitzpatrick stratification, code released.

papers.title: Federated Learning for Privacy-Preserving Skin Lesion Classifier Across 18 Dermatology Clinics
papers.abstract: Dermoscopy AI benefits from multi-site diversity. We implement federated learning to train a skin lesion classifier across 18 clinics without sharing images. AUC 0.93 for melanoma detection is achieved, matching centralised training. The federated model generalises better across skin tones than single-site models. We note this is a federated training methodology paper; the diagnostic model uses a published architecture.
papers.topic_hint: federated learning, dermoscopy, melanoma, multi-site, skin lesion
papers.methodology_hint: federated learning, 18 clinics, AUC 0.93, methodology paper — no new diagnostic architecture
papers.claimed_contribution: Federated dermoscopy melanoma classification AUC 0.93, 18 clinics, methodology contribution.

papers.title: Self-Supervised MoCo Pre-training on 2M Dermoscopy Images for Skin Lesion Classification
papers.abstract: We apply MoCo contrastive pre-training on 2 million unlabelled dermoscopy images before fine-tuning for 14 skin lesion classes. AUC 0.92 averaged across classes is achieved with 100 labelled images per class, outperforming ImageNet pre-training and a fully supervised baseline on the same label budget. An ablation of pre-training duration and data augmentation strategy is provided.
papers.topic_hint: MoCo, self-supervised, dermoscopy, skin lesion, label-efficient
papers.methodology_hint: MoCo pre-training, 2M dermoscopy, AUC 0.92 at 100 labels, ImageNet and supervised baselines, ablation
papers.claimed_contribution: MoCo pre-training for skin lesion classification AUC 0.92 at 100 labels, 2 baselines, ablation.

papers.title: Survival Analysis for Melanoma-Specific Mortality Risk Using Clinical and Dermoscopic Features
papers.abstract: Stage I melanoma has variable prognosis. We train a neural survival model on Breslow thickness, mitotic rate, and dermoscopic features from 1,800 stage I melanoma patients to predict 5-year melanoma-specific mortality. C-index 0.78 is achieved. This is a prognostic model; dermoscopy is used as a feature source, not for initial diagnosis.
papers.topic_hint: neural survival model, melanoma prognosis, dermoscopic features, stage I
papers.methodology_hint: neural survival, C-index 0.78, dermoscopy as features for prognosis — not lesion classification
papers.claimed_contribution: Neural survival model for melanoma prognosis C-index 0.78, dermoscopy features, not diagnosis.

papers.title: GAN-Based Dermoscopy Augmentation for Rare Lesion Type Training
papers.abstract: Rare dermatological lesion classes have insufficient training data. We train a class-conditional GAN on ISIC images to synthesise dermoscopy images of five rare lesion types. Classifiers trained with GAN augmentation improve macro-F1 for rare classes from 0.51 to 0.72. The GAN improves diagnostic classifier performance for rare classes.
papers.topic_hint: GAN, dermoscopy augmentation, rare lesion, ISIC, skin classification
papers.methodology_hint: conditional GAN, ISIC, macro-F1 0.72 for rare classes, augmentation methodology contributing to diagnosis
papers.claimed_contribution: GAN dermoscopy augmentation improving rare lesion classification macro-F1 to 0.72.

papers.title: ResNet-50 for Acne Severity Grading from Clinical Smartphone Photographs
papers.abstract: Acne severity grading guides treatment decisions. We train ResNet-50 on 8,000 standardised clinical facial photographs to classify acne severity across four IGA grades. Weighted kappa 0.78 is achieved, outperforming a dermatologist panel. This uses clinical photographs, not dermoscopy.
papers.topic_hint: ResNet-50, acne grading, clinical photographs, IGA, smartphone
papers.methodology_hint: ResNet-50, 8k clinical photos, kappa 0.78, dermatologist comparison, clinical not dermoscopy
papers.claimed_contribution: ResNet for acne severity grading kappa 0.78, clinical photos not dermoscopy.

papers.title: Transformer for Psoriasis Area and Severity Index Estimation from Body Photographs
papers.abstract: PASI scoring requires time-consuming clinical assessment. We train a transformer on 6,200 full-body photographs to estimate PASI scores, achieving mean absolute error 1.8 PASI units. Correlation with dermatologist PASI is 0.88. This uses clinical photographs for severity estimation, not dermoscopy for lesion classification.
papers.topic_hint: transformer, PASI, psoriasis, body photographs, severity estimation
papers.methodology_hint: transformer, 6200 body photos, MAE 1.8 PASI, severity estimation — not dermoscopy lesion classification
papers.claimed_contribution: Transformer for PASI estimation from body photos, MAE 1.8, not dermoscopy diagnosis.

papers.title: ViT-L/16 for Multiclass Dermoscopy Diagnosis with Uncertainty-Guided Human-AI Collaboration
papers.abstract: We fine-tune ViT-L/16 on 60,000 dermoscopy images for 14-class skin lesion diagnosis. Macro-AUC 0.94 is achieved. A Monte Carlo Dropout uncertainty mechanism routes low-confidence cases to dermatologist review, improving overall workflow accuracy to 0.97 in a simulated hybrid system. Ablation confirms uncertainty routing contributes beyond pure classification. External validation on a multi-centre European dataset is provided.
papers.topic_hint: ViT, dermoscopy, multiclass diagnosis, uncertainty, human-AI collaboration
papers.methodology_hint: ViT-L/16, 60k dermoscopy, macro-AUC 0.94, MC Dropout, hybrid workflow, ablation, external validation
papers.claimed_contribution: ViT for 14-class dermoscopy macro-AUC 0.94, uncertainty routing to 0.97 hybrid, ablation, external validation.

papers.title: Knowledge Distillation for On-Device Skin Lesion Triage in Primary Care
papers.abstract: Deploying dermoscopy AI in primary care requires lightweight models. We distil EfficientNet-B5 to a lightweight student achieving AUC 0.90 for benign/malignant dermoscopy triage at 3x faster inference on smartphone CPUs. Comparison against the teacher model and a pruning baseline is provided.
papers.topic_hint: knowledge distillation, dermoscopy, skin lesion triage, mobile, AUC
papers.methodology_hint: distillation from EfficientNet-B5, AUC 0.90, 3x speedup, pruning baseline, dermoscopy triage
papers.claimed_contribution: Knowledge distillation for dermoscopy triage AUC 0.90, 3x speedup, mobile deployment.

papers.title: Deep Learning for Eczema Patch Testing Result Prediction from Photograph
papers.abstract: Predicting patch test outcomes could guide allergen panels. We train a CNN on 3,200 patch test photographs to predict test result grading. Accuracy 0.84 is achieved. This uses clinical photographs to predict patch test results, not dermoscopy for skin lesion classification.
papers.topic_hint: CNN, eczema, patch testing, clinical photograph, allergen
papers.methodology_hint: CNN, patch test photos, accuracy 0.84 — clinical not dermoscopy, patch test not lesion diagnosis
papers.claimed_contribution: CNN for patch test outcome prediction accuracy 0.84, not dermoscopy lesion diagnosis.

papers.title: Meta-Analysis of Deep Learning for Melanoma Detection: 62 Studies
papers.abstract: We meta-analyse 62 studies on deep learning for melanoma detection from dermoscopy. Pooled sensitivity 0.88 and specificity 0.82 are reported. Substantial heterogeneity in dataset characteristics and validation design is found. No new model is developed.
papers.topic_hint: meta-analysis, melanoma detection, dermoscopy, 62 studies
papers.methodology_hint: meta-analysis, 62 studies, no new model
papers.claimed_contribution: Meta-analysis of 62 DL melanoma detection studies, heterogeneity analysis, no new model.

papers.title: U-Net for Dermoscopic Lesion Boundary Segmentation Without Classification
papers.abstract: Accurate lesion boundary segmentation supports clinical measurement and feature extraction. We train a U-Net on 4,000 manually annotated dermoscopy images to segment lesion boundaries. Dice 0.91 is achieved. The model segments boundaries only and does not classify lesion type or malignancy.
papers.topic_hint: U-Net, dermoscopy, lesion segmentation, boundary delineation
papers.methodology_hint: U-Net, 4000 dermoscopy images, Dice 0.91, segmentation only — no classification
papers.claimed_contribution: U-Net dermoscopy lesion segmentation Dice 0.91, no lesion classification.

papers.title: Dense CNN for Reflectance Confocal Microscopy Basal Cell Carcinoma Detection
papers.abstract: Reflectance confocal microscopy provides near-histological skin imaging. We train a dense CNN on 3,800 RCM sequences from 420 patients to detect basal cell carcinoma. Sensitivity 0.89 at specificity 0.88 is achieved, outperforming dermoscopy expert assessment. RCM enables non-invasive BCC diagnosis.
papers.topic_hint: dense CNN, reflectance confocal microscopy, BCC, skin lesion detection
papers.methodology_hint: dense CNN, RCM 3800 sequences, sensitivity 0.89, dermoscopy expert comparison
papers.claimed_contribution: Dense CNN for RCM-based BCC detection sensitivity 0.89, dermoscopy expert comparison.

papers.title: ABCDE Feature-Guided Attention CNN for Melanoma Diagnosis with Explainability
papers.abstract: Clinical ABCDE criteria for melanoma assessment provide structured explainability anchors. We incorporate ABCDE-derived attention supervision into a CNN trained on 32,000 dermoscopy images. Balanced accuracy 0.87 is achieved for melanoma diagnosis, with attention maps validated against dermatologist feature ratings. The model outperforms a standard fine-tuned CNN baseline and provides interpretable diagnostic signals.
papers.topic_hint: ABCDE, attention CNN, melanoma diagnosis, dermoscopy, explainability
papers.methodology_hint: ABCDE-guided attention CNN, 32k dermoscopy, balanced accuracy 0.87, CNN baseline, dermatologist validation
papers.claimed_contribution: ABCDE-guided attention CNN for melanoma diagnosis 0.87, CNN baseline, dermatologist-validated attention.

papers.title: Reinforcement Learning for Biopsy Site Selection in Suspicious Skin Lesions
papers.abstract: When biopsying a large or complex skin lesion, optimal site selection maximises diagnostic yield. We train an RL agent on dermoscopy images to select biopsy coordinates. Diagnostic yield improves by 18% in simulation. This is a procedural guidance tool, not automated skin lesion classification.
papers.topic_hint: RL, biopsy site selection, skin lesion, procedural guidance
papers.methodology_hint: RL, biopsy site selection, dermoscopy — procedural guidance not classification
papers.claimed_contribution: RL for optimal biopsy site selection, 18% diagnostic yield improvement, not classification.

papers.title: Contrastive Learning for Cross-Domain Dermoscopy Generalisation Across Camera Types
papers.abstract: Dermoscopy AI is sensitive to camera and acquisition protocol. We apply cross-domain contrastive learning to align features across six dermoscope camera types, training on ISIC and a proprietary dataset. Generalisation AUC improves from 0.83 to 0.91 on held-out camera types for 8-class skin lesion diagnosis. Ablation confirms domain alignment is more effective than simple augmentation.
papers.topic_hint: contrastive learning, dermoscopy, domain generalisation, camera types, skin lesion
papers.methodology_hint: contrastive cross-domain, 6 camera types, AUC 0.91, ablation vs augmentation, skin lesion classification
papers.claimed_contribution: Contrastive dermoscopy domain generalisation AUC 0.91 across camera types, ablation.

papers.title: Clinical Trial for Topical Retinoid Efficacy in Acne: No AI Component
papers.abstract: A randomised controlled trial evaluating topical tretinoin versus adapalene for moderate acne. IGA response at 12 weeks is the primary endpoint. No machine learning or automated image analysis is used.
papers.topic_hint: RCT, acne, retinoid, clinical trial
papers.methodology_hint: RCT, no ML, no image analysis
papers.claimed_contribution: RCT for topical retinoid acne treatment, no AI component.

papers.title: ViT for Nail Psoriasis Severity Scoring from Standardised Nail Photographs
papers.abstract: Nail psoriasis is common and hard to quantify. We train ViT-B/16 on 4,100 standardised nail photographs to score NAPSI severity. AUC 0.88 versus expert NAPSI is achieved. This uses clinical photographs of nails, not dermoscopy for skin lesion classification.
papers.topic_hint: ViT, nail psoriasis, NAPSI, nail photographs, severity scoring
papers.methodology_hint: ViT-B/16, nail photos, AUC 0.88, nail psoriasis severity — not dermoscopy lesion diagnosis
papers.claimed_contribution: ViT for NAPSI nail psoriasis scoring AUC 0.88, not skin lesion classification.

papers.title: Sparse Autoencoder for Unsupervised Discovery of Dermoscopic Lesion Subtypes
papers.abstract: Unsupervised dermoscopy analysis may reveal novel lesion subtypes. We train a sparse autoencoder on 80,000 dermoscopy images and cluster the latent space into 24 groups. Downstream classification of clinically labelled cases achieves AUC 0.87. The pipeline ends in supervised classification of dermoscopy images.
papers.topic_hint: sparse autoencoder, dermoscopy, unsupervised subtype discovery, classification
papers.methodology_hint: sparse autoencoder, 80k dermoscopy, 24 clusters, AUC 0.87 downstream classification
papers.claimed_contribution: Sparse autoencoder for dermoscopy subtype discovery, downstream classification AUC 0.87.

papers.title: Bayesian CNN for Dermoscopy with Calibrated Uncertainty for Triage in Teledermatology
papers.abstract: Teledermatology triage requires both accurate diagnosis and reliable uncertainty. We train a Bayesian CNN using MC Dropout on 18,000 dermoscopy images for 6-class skin lesion diagnosis. AUC 0.91 is achieved. The model's uncertainty estimates stratify cases by risk in a teledermatology simulation, with low-confidence cases achieving equal accuracy to dermatologist consensus after expert review. Comparison against a deterministic EfficientNet baseline is provided.
papers.topic_hint: Bayesian CNN, dermoscopy, teledermatology, uncertainty, 6-class diagnosis
papers.methodology_hint: Bayesian CNN, 18k dermoscopy, AUC 0.91, EfficientNet baseline, teledermatology simulation
papers.claimed_contribution: Bayesian CNN for teledermatology dermoscopy diagnosis AUC 0.91 with uncertainty triage, EfficientNet comparison.
